title: prunus pumila subsp. besseyi
intro: Prunus pumila subsp. besseyi, also known as the sand cherry, is a deciduous shrub native to North America, characterized by its small white flowers and edible red fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of sand cherry have been found to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and protecting against certain types of cancer. Sand cherry is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
